subject: Biology
title: Cell Signaling
description: A fast breakdown of Cell Signaling for builders.

hook.text: Cells send and receive chemical messages constantly. Your body runs on molecular conversations.

buildup.text: A signal molecule binds a receptor on the membrane, triggering a chain reaction inside.

discovery.text: Insulin binds cell receptors and tells them to absorb glucose from the blood.

twist.text: Cancer often hijacks signaling pathways, telling cells to grow when they shouldn't.

climax.text: From hormones to neurotransmitters, nearly every body function depends on cell signaling.

punchline.text: Cells never stop talking to each other.

quiz.question: How does insulin affect cells?
options: It signals them to absorb glucose
